jurisdictional_context:
Germany has crossed an institutional inflection point on the so-called Parental Alienation construct (PAS / Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung) between 2023 and 2026. The apex reference is BVerfG 17.11.2023 — 1 BvR 1076/23, in which the First Senate characterised PAS / Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung as an *uberkommene und fachwissenschaftlich als widerlegt geltende* construct and held that an Umgangsregelung cannot be justified by a one-sided PAS-framing without a Gesamtwurdigung anchored in the Kindeswohl. This is the highest-rank German constitutional-court signal against PAS-as-diagnosis to date (see case-study:bverfg-1-bvr-1076-23-germany-2023). The BVerfG explicitly drew on the Deutsches Jugendinstitut (DJI)-anchored Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) ZKJ critique 'Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen' as scientific authority — meaning the critique-camp scholars are the BVerfG's own cited authority, not a minority position.

The constitutional line has since been reinforced by OLG Frankfurt am Main 05.01.2026 — 7 UF 88/25, which both labelled PAS a *pseudowissenschaftliche Hypothese* and imposed Sachverstandigen-Mindestanforderungen (multimodaler Ansatz, no one-sided selection of interview partners) for any Gutachten touching alleged alienation dynamics (see case-study:olg-frankfurt-7-uf-88-25-germany-2026). The statutory baseline remains §§ 1684 BGB (Umgangsforderungspflicht / Wohlverhaltensgebot) and 1696 BGB (Abanderung fur triftige Grunde), with FamFG §159 on the Kindesanhorung and the BGH 1980 / 2009 line treating Umgangsausschluss as ultima ratio. Practitioner regulation runs through Approbation als Psychologische:r oder Arztliche:r Psychotherapeut:in (PsychThG-Reform 2020), Kammerregistrierung bei der jeweiligen Landes-Psychotherapeutenkammer, and — for forensic Sachverstandige — Fachpsycholog:in fur Rechtspsychologie BDP/DGPs.

Three historical / institutional caveats anchor the directory. First, the Cochemer Modell (Familiengericht Cochem-Zell 1992, Richter Jurgen Rudolph) was integrated conceptually into the 2009 FamFG-Reform, but Cochem-Zell itself discontinued the Modell after Rudolph's 2008 retirement — it survives as an exportable label, not as a living local practice. Second, PETRA — the operational vehicle of the recognition camp's most cited contemporary German voice, Stefan Rucker — operates from PETRA Projekt GmbH & Co. KG based in Bremen and NOT from the Universitat Bremen; the Universitat Bremen Senat publicly disputed the framing 'PETRA Arbeitsgruppe an der Universitat Bremen' in 2024 and requested correction. Third, the Bundesarbeitsgemeinschaft Eltern und Kind (BAG-EUK) named in activist literature is not Vereinsregister-verifiable at directory standard. The Istanbul-Konvention Art. 31 and the GREVIO Germany Baseline Report 2022 form the supranational anchor of the critique camp.
structural_findings: Germany 2023-26 has crossed an institutional inflection point on the PAS construct: the BVerfG 17.11.2023 — 1 BvR 1076/23 ruling, combined with the DJI-anchored Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) ZKJ critique and the OLG Frankfurt 7 UF 88/25 (5.1.2026) Sachverstandigen-Mindestanforderungen, has moved the critique camp from minority position to institutional mainstream. The German PA-clinical scene is structurally inverted compared with most other PASG-active jurisdictions: the recognition camp is counter-mainstream, the critique camp is institutional core.

Critique-camp scholars are the BVerfG's own cited authority: the Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) ZKJ team is not an external commentariat but the scientific reference the Federal Constitutional Court itself relied on. This makes German critique-camp clinicians uniquely well-anchored relative to peers in other EU jurisdictions.

The recognition camp's older anchors (Boch-Galhau, Kodjoe, Andritzky, Jopt) operate without an institutional university-clinic base. The contemporary recognition-camp lead voice, Stefan Rucker, operates from PETRA Projekt GmbH & Co. KG (private vehicle) — NOT from the Universitat Bremen, whose Senat publicly disputed the 'PETRA Arbeitsgruppe an der Universitat Bremen' framing in 2024 and requested correction.

Jorg Fichtner is critique-camp, not recognition-camp — frequently miscategorised by external observers because of his forensic Sachverstandigen practice; he is in fact a co-signatory of the central ZKJ 2023 critique article cited by the BVerfG.
Cochem-Zell no longer practices the Cochemer Modell — model was discontinued at the Cochem Familiengericht after Rudolph's 2008 retirement and survives as an exportable method label only.

No individual German forensic Sachverstandige is repeatedly positively-named in post-2023 OLG PA case-law: the case-law trend is the systematic criticism of Gutachter-qualifications in PA-framed custody-transfer recommendations, codified by OLG Frankfurt 7 UF 88/25 (2026).

Reinhart Wolff is excluded from the directory on safeguarding grounds (publicly documented historical position sympathetic to child-adult sexual contact disqualifies him from a child-protection-aligned PA directory). Listed in excluded[] with primary-source pointer.

entries.name: Dr. med. Wilfrid von Boch-Galhau
entries.credentials: Facharzt fur Psychiatrie und Neurologie / Psychotherapeut (Approbation)
entries.affiliation: Private practice Wurzburg-Steinbachtal; long-standing PASG / EAPAP contributor
entries.stance_notes: Foundational German PA-recognition voice: editor and co-translator of the German edition of Gardner (2002), monograph *Parental Alienation und Parental Alienation Syndrome/Disorder* (VWB-Verlag 2013), and 2018 international journal article framing PA(S) as a serious form of psychological child abuse. Position is PASG-aligned classical recognition.

publications.title: Parental Alienation und Parental Alienation Syndrome/Disorder
Gardner: Das elterliche Entfremdungssyndrom (deutsche Ubersetzung)
Parental Alienation (Syndrome) — A serious form of psychological child abuse

entries.verification_notes: Practice listed as active per October 2025 directory updates; cross-listed in /influencers/wilfrid-von-boch-galhau.md. Historical anchor of the German PA-recognition literature.

entries.name: Dipl.-Psych. Ursula Kodjoe
entries.affiliation: Independent practice Emmendingen; PASG external resources contributor
entries.stance_notes: Co-initiator (with Koeppel, late 1990s) of the German PAS-Arbeitsgemeinschaft. 2018 PASG Stockholm presentation advocates a Consensus Model in Germany with interdisciplinary cooperation; recurring expert voice in NZZ / Spiegel coverage of Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung. Position is PASG-aligned recognition with consensus-model bridge to mediation.

publications.title: Kodjoe & Koeppel — initiation of the German PAS-Debate (PAS-Arbeitsgemeinschaft)
Consensus Model in Germany with interdisciplinary cooperation (PASG Stockholm presentation)
PA / PAS Update

entries.contact: LinkedIn DM (institutional verification path); specific street address not publicly confirmed
entries.verification_notes: Recurring expert in German press coverage (NZZ, Spiegel) on Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung; bridge figure between recognition camp and consensus-model mediation.

entries.name: Dr. phil. Walter Andritzky
entries.affiliation: Independent researcher; not currently affiliated with a verifiable university chair or clinic
entries.stance_notes: Author of the 2002 Psychotherapie article *Verhaltensmuster und Personlichkeitsstruktur entfremdender Eltern* and the 2002 Deutsches Arzteblatt note *Nicht instrumentalisieren*, plus 2011 PA/PAS Update co-authored with Boch-Galhau / Kodjoe / Koeppel. Position is recognition-camp via published articles; PASG-aligned.

publications.title: Verhaltensmuster und Personlichkeitsstruktur entfremdender Eltern
Nicht instrumentalisieren
PA/PAS Update

entries.verification_notes: Chamber registration not publicly confirmed; active clinical practice status not currently verifiable. Retained as historical anchor of the German PA-recognition literature.

entries.name: Dr. Stefan Jurgen Rucker
entries.credentials: Dr. (Psychologe / Kinderpsychologe); leads PETRA Projekt GmbH & Co. KG (private vehicle); ZKPR Universitat Bremen Kooperation bis 2020
entries.affiliation: PETRA Projekt GmbH & Co. KG, Bremen (private operating vehicle; NOT a Universitat Bremen chair — Senat publicly disputed the institutional framing in 2024)
entries.stance_notes: Co-author of the two-part ZKJ 2022 article *Zur Notwendigkeit professioneller Intervention bei Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung* (Baumann, Michel-Biegel, Rucker, Serafin & Wiesner 2022) — the central contemporary German PA-recognition-camp clinical-intervention paper. Coordinator of the BMJV-commissioned PETRA / ZKPR Uni Bremen 2022/23 study *Kindeswohl und Umgangsrecht*. Position is recognition camp; pro-intervention.

publications.title: Zur Notwendigkeit professioneller Intervention bei Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung Teil 1 & 2
publications.venue: ZKJ 7/2022 pp. 244-252 and 8/2022 pp. 292-298 (Baumann, Michel-Biegel, Rucker, Serafin & Wiesner)

entries.contact: Via PETRA Projekt GmbH & Co. KG, Bremen
entries.verification_notes: Major correction vs. surface assumption — Rucker is NOT a Universitatsprofessor at Bremen; Universitat Bremen Senat (2024) disputed the institutional framing of a PETRA-Arbeitsgruppe and requested correction. Recognition camp's most cited contemporary German voice but operates from a private GmbH vehicle.

entries.name: Dipl.-Psych. Dr. Eginhard Walter
entries.credentials: Diplom-Psychologe; Vorstand Institut Gericht & Familie Berlin/Brandenburg e.V.; Dozent in rechtspsychologischer Weiterbildung
entries.affiliation: Institut Gericht & Familie Berlin/Brandenburg e.V.; co-author of Dettenborn & Walter standard textbook (Reinhardt UTB)
entries.stance_notes: Co-author with Harry Dettenborn of *Familienrechtspsychologie* (Reinhardt UTB, 2. Aufl. 2016) — the standard German textbook for forensic family-court psychology, which treats PA / EKE as a recognised clinical reference category. Position is recognition-camp via textbook; methodologically closer to the middle camp than the Boch-Galhau / PASG line.

publications.title: Familienrechtspsychologie
publications.venue: Reinhardt UTB, 2. Aufl. (with Harry Dettenborn) — Standard-Lehrbuch

entries.verification_notes: Recognition-camp via textbook; methodologically closer to the middle camp than the Boch-Galhau / PASG line.

entries.name: Prof. Dr. (em.) Uwe-Jorg Jopt
entries.credentials: Professor em. fur Psychologie, Universitat Bielefeld; psychologischer Sachverstandiger
entries.stance_notes: Author of the foundational German PA Two-Phase Model: Jopt & Behrend (2000) *Das Parental Alienation Syndrome (PAS) — Ein Zwei-Phasen-Modell*, Zentralblatt fur Jugendrecht 87(7-8). Earlier 1992 monograph *Im Namen des Kindes* (Rasch & Rohring) argued for the abolition of sole custody — proto-bigenitorial position. Position is PASG-aligned recognition.

publications.title: Das Parental Alienation Syndrome (PAS) — Ein Zwei-Phasen-Modell
Im Namen des Kindes — Pladoyer fur die Abschaffung des alleinigen Sorgerechts

entries.verification_notes: Foundational German PA-recognition academic. Active clinical practice status not currently verifiable; entry retained as historical anchor of the German two-phase PAS model.

entries.name: Prof. Dr. Heinz Kindler, Dipl.-Psych.
entries.credentials: Honorarprofessor; Diplom-Psychologe; Doctorate 2001 Universitat Regensburg; Leiter Fachgruppe F3 Familienhilfe und Kinderschutz, Deutsches Jugendinstitut (DJI) seit 2012
entries.affiliation: Deutsches Jugendinstitut (DJI) Munich — Leiter Fachgruppe F3 Familienhilfe und Kinderschutz
entries.stance_notes: Co-signatory of the central German critique-camp article Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) *Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen*, ZKJ 2023 — the scientific authority directly cited by BVerfG 17.11.2023 — 1 BvR 1076/23. NZZ 2024 quotes Kindler's family-law psychological assessment sample analysis showing that the relationship with the alleged manipulating parent worsens through manipulation attempts. Single most-cited German child-protection scientist on PA-scepticism.

publications.title: Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen — Warum wir allzu vereinfachte Vorstellungen von Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung hinter uns lassen mussen
Family-law psychological assessment sample analysis (cited NZZ 2024)

entries.verification_notes: Single most-cited German child-protection scientist on PA-scepticism; directly cited by the BVerfG-aligned line of reasoning.

entries.name: Prof. Dr. Sabine Walper
entries.credentials: Prof. Dr. (Habilitation LMU 1999); Lehrstuhl Allgemeine Padagogik LMU Munchen (beurlaubt seit 2012); Direktorin und Vorstandsvorsitzende Deutsches Jugendinstitut (DJI) seit 01.10.2021
entries.affiliation: Deutsches Jugendinstitut (DJI) Munich (Direktorin); LMU Munchen, Lehrstuhl Allgemeine Padagogik (beurlaubt)
entries.stance_notes: DJI Direktorin and co-signatory of Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) *Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen* ZKJ 2023 — directly cited by BVerfG 17.11.2023 — 1 BvR 1076/23. Editor of DJI Impulse 3+4/2024 *Elternkonflikte meistern*. Walper's signature carries the institutional weight of the BMFSFJ's central family-research institute and gives the critique camp its institutional authority in German family-court discourse.

publications.title: Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen

entries.name: Dr. Jorg Fichtner
entries.credentials: Diplom-Psychologe; Dr. (1998); Fachpsychologe fur Rechtspsychologie BDP/DGPs; forensischer Sachverstandiger; Mediator
entries.affiliation: Private practice Munich (joerg-fichtner.de); past DJI Hochkonflikthaftigkeit project lead author 2010
entries.stance_notes: Co-signatory of Zimmermann / Fichtner / Walper / Lux / Kindler (2023) *Verdorbener Wein in neuen Schlauchen* ZKJ 2023 — the article cited by BVerfG 17.11.2023 — 1 BvR 1076/23. Lead author of the DJI 2010 *Hochkonflikthaftigkeit in Trennungsfamilien — Wissenschaftlicher Abschlussbericht*. Major correction vs. surface assumption: Fichtner is frequently miscategorised as recognition camp because of his forensic family-court Sachverstandigen practice, but he is in fact a co-signatory of the principal German PAS-critique article cited by the BVerfG.

entries.name: Dr. phil. Wolfgang Hammer
entries.credentials: Dr. phil. (Soziologie / Padagogik / Ethnologie, Universitaten Hamburg & Koln); ehemals Leitung Hamburger Landesjugendamt; Vertreter der Jugend- und Familienministerkonferenz 2005-2013; Wissenschaftlicher Beirat Deutsches Kinderhilfswerk seit 2017
entries.affiliation: familienrecht-in-deutschland.de (independent platform); Wissenschaftlicher Beirat Deutsches Kinderhilfswerk
entries.stance_notes: Author of the Hammer et al. November 2024 empirical study *Macht und Kontrolle in familienrechtlichen Verfahren in Deutschland — Eine Analyse medialer Falldokumentationen* (154 cases / 269 sources, MAXQDA) — the empirical reference cited by Frauenhauskoordinierung, VAMV and the GREVIO follow-up debate. Position is critique camp via documented sociological / empirical critique of family-court practice. Not a clinical psychotherapist — sociologist / former Jugendamt official, included as non-clinical critique-camp anchor of 2024-26 German PA discourse.

publications.title: Macht und Kontrolle in familienrechtlichen Verfahren in Deutschland — Eine Analyse medialer Falldokumentationen (154 Falle / 269 Quellen, MAXQDA)

entries.name: Frauenhauskoordinierung e.V.

entries.stance_notes: Institutional anchor of the critique camp's organisational centre of gravity. FHK 2022 press statement on the GREVIO report; July 2024 substantive note on Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung (PAS) und Wunsch nach Sanktionierung citing BVerfG 1 BvR 1076/23; May 2025 Muttertag press release framing Gewaltschutz as priority over Sorge- und Umgangsrecht. Position: critique camp institutional anchor.

publications.title: Pressemeldung zum GREVIO-Bericht: Deutschland braucht Gesamtkonzept gegen Gewalt an Frauen
Beitrag: Eltern-Kind-Entfremdung (PAS) und Wunsch nach Sanktionierung — BVerfG 1 BvR 1076/23
Pressemitteilung zum Muttertag: Mehr Schutz fur Mutter — Gewaltschutz muss Vorrang vor Sorge- und Umgangsrecht haben

entries.verification_notes: Institutional entry, not an individual practitioner — included because the critique camp's organisational centre of gravity sits with FHK rather than with any individual clinician.

entries.name: Christoph Cornelius Paul
entries.credentials: Rechtsanwalt und Notar a.D.; Mediator BAFM; Bundesverdienstkreuz am Bande
entries.affiliation: MiKK e.V. — Internationales Mediationszentrum fur Familienkonflikte und Kindesentfuhrung (founder / patron)
entries.stance_notes: Founder (2002 / 2012) and patron of MiKK e.V., Germany's principal cross-border family-mediation institutional structure. MiKK works PA dynamics routinely as part of high-conflict cross-border custody and Hague-1980 child-abduction mediation but does not frame them as a single named syndrome. Position is middle camp: methodological mediation focus, no PASG alignment, no public critique-camp signature.

publications.title: Founder and patron of MiKK e.V. — Internationales Mediationszentrum fur Familienkonflikte und Kindesentfuhrung

entries.verification_notes: Not a psychologist — included as patron and founding voice of Germany's principal middle-camp institutional structure on cross-border family conflict, in which PA dynamics are routinely worked but not framed as a single named syndrome.

entries.name: Ischtar Khalaf-Newsome & Magdalena Janczuk (MiKK Co-CEOs)
entries.credentials: Khalaf-Newsome: Family Lawyer, Mediator. Janczuk: M.A., Mediator.
entries.affiliation: MiKK e.V. — Internationales Mediationszentrum fur Familienkonflikte und Kindesentfuhrung (operational co-leadership)
entries.stance_notes: Operational co-leadership of MiKK e.V.'s cross-border family-mediation programme (~150 inquiries/year, ~40 mediations, 80% success). Position is middle camp: mediation-foregrounded interface working PA dynamics without naming PAS, Hague-1980 / Brussels-IIb operative context.

publications.title: Operational leadership of MiKK e.V. cross-border family-mediation programme (~150 inquiries/year, ~40 mediations, 80% success)

entries.verification_notes: Listed jointly as the current operational middle-camp interface. Clinical credentials limited; legal / mediation foreground.

entries.name: Jurgen Rudolph (Richter a.D.)
entries.credentials: Familienrichter a.D. (Amtsgericht Cochem until 2008); Autor; internationaler Referent zu Kinderrecht und Familienkonfliktbearbeitung
entries.affiliation: Amtsgericht Cochem (a.D.); independent author / international lecturer
entries.stance_notes: Originator of the Cochemer Modell (Familiengericht Cochem-Zell 1992-2008) — interdisciplinary cooperation between Familiengericht, Jugendamt, Beratungsstellen and Rechtsanwalten, conceptually absorbed into the 2009 FamFG-Reform. Author of *Du bist mein Kind — Die Cochemer Praxis* (Schwarzkopf & Schwarzkopf 2007). Position is middle camp: methodological / interdisciplinary focus, no PASG alignment. Important correction: Cochem-Zell no longer practices the Cochemer Modell — discontinued at the Cochem Familiengericht after Rudolph's 2008 retirement; survives as exportable method label only.
